meaning: n.鍑忓皯鐩愬垎,鑴辩洂浣滅敤
examples: Some desalination plants exist where feasible, but they are costly to run and can need constant repair.
This means that some wells may even need to undergo desalination after extraction, thus increasing the cost.
A refuse incinerator commissioned in 1993 used to contribute heat to help run the desalination system.
Furthermore, even 20 desalination plants would only add a cup to California's water bucket.
Environmental issues aside, the biggest challenge for Poseidon, and desalination in general, is cost.
